results.possibleIdentity: Vincent T. Murphy was a technical researcher and physicist associated with Bell Telephone Laboratories (Bell Labs) in Murray Hill, New Jersey, during the late 1960s and 1970s. His work focused on experimental physics and materials science, specifically investigating ion bombardment, ion neutralization processes, and the electrical properties of silicon dioxide (SiO2) films. He co-authored several significant technical papers with colleagues like Daniel V. McCaughan and R. A. Kushner, including research on low-energy ion bombardment of silicon dioxide films on silicon (1973) and ion-etch pattern definition (1976). His technical fields included physics, X-ray analysis, and computer programming for data analysis and modeling. Murphy is also noted for his potential association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that fostered collaborations between the technical and artistic communities; his index card specifically highlights interests or activities in computer graphics and film, common areas for E.A.T. projects at Bell Labs. He likely held a physics degree, with records showing a Vincent T. Murphy graduated in physics from Arlington State College in 1961.
